stories.title: Synopsys debuts AI chip design tools at Converge 2026
stories.description: The launches mark the first major product deliveries since Synopsys completed its $35 billion acquisition of Ansys last year.

stories.title: Oil prices climb again as nations plan historic reserve release
stories.description: Crude rose more than 3% Wednesday despite governments signaling emergency stockpile releases amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

stories.title: U.S. military probe finds America at fault in Iran school strike that killed 175
stories.description: Outdated targeting data led to the Tomahawk missile hitting a girls' elementary school on the war's first day, according to preliminary findings.

stories.title: Norway arrests three brothers over U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo
stories.description: The suspects, all Norwegian citizens of Iraqi background in their twenties, face terror bombing charges under the penal code.

stories.title: Kilauea's 1,000-foot lava fountains force closures on Big Island
stories.description: Episode 43 of the ongoing eruption showered communities with volcanic debris, closing Highway 11 and parts of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park.

stories.title: Trump says 'practically nothing left' to target in Iran
stories.description: Israel's defense minister vowed to fight on "without any time limit," while Iran warned of a prolonged war of attrition.

stories.title: IRGC claims strikes on ships in Strait of Hormuz, demands transit approval
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it targeted vessels that ignored warnings, as three cargo ships were hit by projectiles on Wednesday.

stories.title: IEA confirms largest-ever oil reserve release at 400M barrels
stories.description: The unprecedented action aims to contain surging energy prices as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran has effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Anthropic launches policy institute amid Pentagon legal battle
stories.description: Co-founder Jack Clark will lead the Anthropic Institute, a new research body studying AI's impact on jobs, law, and the economy.

stories.title: Trump to visit Ohio, Kentucky to sell economic message and campaign against Massie
stories.description: The president's two-stop swing marks the first time this cycle he has personally campaigned to oust a sitting Republican member of Congress.

stories.title: Iran's president's son says new leader is 'safe and sound' amid injury reports
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since being named supreme leader on March 9, fueling speculation about wounds from U.S.-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Von der Leyen calls return to Russian energy 'a strategic blunder' amid Iran war
stories.description: The European Commission president told the European Parliament that 10 days of conflict have already cost EU taxpayers an extra €3 billion in fossil fuel imports.

stories.title: US gas prices top $3.50 as Iran war disrupts Strait of Hormuz oil flow
stories.description: The national average hit $3.54 per gallon as of March 10, up more than 60 cents in a month, with California topping $5.29.

stories.title: UN Security Council to vote on resolution demanding Iran halt Gulf attacks
stories.description: The Gulf-backed measure, with over 90 co-sponsors, condemns Iran's strikes on neighbors and its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: New AI tools compress months of drug and protein research into days
stories.description: From drug synthesis to protein mapping, multiple studies published in recent weeks show machine learning dramatically cutting lab time and costs.

stories.title: Trial opens for 4 men charged in Haiti president's assassination
stories.description: Prosecutors told the jury that greed and a thirst for power drove the South Florida-based conspiracy to kill Jovenel Moïse in 2021.

stories.title: AG Bondi relocates to military base amid cartel, Epstein threats
stories.description: The attorney general quietly left her D.C. apartment for a nearby military installation, joining a growing list of Trump officials seeking base housing.

stories.title: Experts accuse Bukele's government of crimes against humanity
stories.description: International experts filed a complaint with the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleging systematic torture, killings, and disappearances under El Salvador's four-year emergency rule.

stories.title: Fuller and Harris head to Georgia runoff for Greene seat
stories.description: Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller will face off April 7 for the deep-red 14th District seat.

stories.title: Pentagon orders removal of Anthropic AI from military systems
stories.description: An internal memo directs commanders to strip Claude from nuclear, missile defense, and cyber warfare systems as Anthropic sues the Trump administration.

stories.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Haifa refinery in second attack in three days
stories.description: The Tuesday strikes targeted oil, gas, and fuel storage facilities at Israel's largest refinery, deepening a tit-for-tat energy infrastructure war.

stories.title: Hyde-Smith wins Mississippi GOP Senate primary
stories.description: The Trump-endorsed incumbent defeated challenger Sarah Adlakha with roughly 83% of the vote, advancing to the November general election.

stories.title: Johnson opposes nation-building in Iran, breaks with Trump
stories.description: The House speaker said rebuilding Iran is "not our responsibility," a day after the president called the war "the beginning of building a new country."

stories.title: Sen. Blumenthal warns US is on path to ground troops in Iran
stories.description: After a classified briefing, the senator said Russia appears to be "actively and intensively" aiding Iran as the Pentagon reported 140 U.S. troops wounded.

stories.title: Israel says it's not seeking 'endless war' as strikes on Tehran intensify
stories.description: Foreign Minister Sa'ar pledged to coordinate with the U.S. on ending the conflict but offered no timeline on day 11 of fighting.

stories.title: IRGC says Iran will decide when war ends, threatens Gulf oil blockade
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards dismissed Trump's claims the conflict is nearly over, warning they would halt all regional oil exports.

stories.title: Sen. Kennedy apologizes for US strike on Iranian girls' school that killed 168
stories.description: The Louisiana Republican broke with Trump, calling the Feb. 28 bombing of the Minab school a "mistake" and expressing sorrow for the deaths.

stories.title: Merz calls Israel's West Bank settlement push a 'big mistake'
stories.description: Germany's foreign minister made a surprise visit to Jerusalem to deliver the message directly, the first by a European diplomat since the Iran war began.

stories.title: US Navy refuses escort requests through Hormuz
stories.description: The Navy has told shipping companies daily that attacks risks are too high for escorts, even as Trump promises tanker protection.

stories.title: Hegseth warns Russia against sharing intel with Iran
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister confirmed Moscow is "helping us in many different directions" as the U.S. accuses Russia of aiding Tehran with military intelligence.

stories.title: Tankers begin running Strait of Hormuz blockade as US backs $20B reinsurance plan
stories.description: A handful of oil tankers have broken through the effective Hormuz blockade, but daily transits remain near zero as Iran warns vessels to stay away.

stories.title: White House urges GOP to drop 'mass deportation' talk
stories.description: Deputy Chief of Staff James Blair told House Republicans to shift messaging toward removing violent criminals as polls show eroding support.

stories.title: Senate confirms Joshua Rudd to lead NSA and Cyber Command
stories.description: The 71-29 vote ends a nearly year-long leadership vacancy atop the nation's top signals intelligence agency and military cyber operations arm.

stories.title: Pentagon warns of 'most intense' day of strikes on Iran
stories.description: On day 11 of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, Defense Secretary Hegseth said Tuesday would see the most fighters, bombers, and strikes yet deployed.

stories.title: Trump announces first new US oil refinery in 50 years
stories.description: India's Reliance Industries is backing the Brownsville, Texas, facility, which will process domestic shale oil into gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel.

stories.title: Musk says X Money will enter early public access in April
stories.description: The digital payment system, built into the X platform, offers P2P transfers, a Visa debit card, and a 6% yield on deposits.

stories.title: Up to 150 US troops wounded in Iran war, Pentagon says
stories.description: The figure, disclosed after a Reuters report, far exceeds the eight serious injuries the Pentagon had previously acknowledged publicly.

stories.title: Witkoff likely to visit Israel next week to coordinate Iran war plans
stories.description: Trump's special envoy plans to visit Israel next week to coordinate the ongoing military campaign as war with Iran enters its second week.

stories.title: Ukraine says leaked Russian documents show 1.3M war losses
stories.description: Classified reports obtained by Ukrainian intelligence put Russia's own count of killed and seriously wounded at 1.315 million, Zelenskyy said.

stories.title: Iran war fuels global debate over shift to renewables
stories.description: EU ministers began emergency energy talks as oil price swings and disrupted shipping routes revive calls for clean energy as a security imperative.

stories.title: NRC proposes cutting nuclear safety inspections by 38% under Trump orders
stories.description: Emergency preparedness inspections face the steepest cuts at 56%, with security and radiation protection hours also sharply reduced.

stories.title: Shots fired at US Consulate in Toronto as attacks on American posts spread
stories.description: Police found roughly 10 shell casings and a damaged door but no injuries after a predawn shooting at the consulate on University Avenue.

stories.title: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square files for $5B dual IPO on NYSE
stories.description: The combined offering would list both a new closed-end fund and Ackman's management company, with shares priced at $50 apiece.

stories.title: Trump advisers privately push for Iran exit strategy amid surging gas prices
stories.description: National gas prices have jumped more than 60 cents in a month as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupts oil flows, raising GOP midterm fears.

stories.title: Netanyahu vows to keep striking Iran, says campaign is 'breaking their bones'
stories.description: Israel has tripled its strike tempo since the U.S.-Israeli offensive began Feb. 28, while Iran vows to fight on and rejects any ceasefire.

stories.title: Lawmakers in 11 states propose temporary bans on data centers
stories.description: Bipartisan legislation targets new data center construction over concerns about soaring electricity costs and water use driven by the AI boom.

stories.title: Apple now assembles 25% of iPhones in India
stories.description: India-made iPhones hit 55 million units in 2025, a 53% jump, as Apple accelerates its manufacturing shift away from China.

stories.title: ALMA detects high methanol levels in interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
stories.description: The third known interstellar object to pass through our solar system has a chemical makeup unlike nearly any comet born here, researchers say.

stories.title: USDA monitors fertilizer market for price gouging amid Iran war supply crisis
stories.description: The department is watching for unfair markups as the Strait of Hormuz closure chokes off shipments weeks before spring planting.

stories.title: Iran's FM rules out new talks with US, citing attacks during negotiations
stories.description: Araghchi told PBS News that diplomacy with Washington is off the table after the US launched strikes days after both sides reported progress in nuclear talks.

stories.title: Macron, IAEA chief open nuclear energy summit in Paris
stories.description: World leaders gathered to discuss expanding civilian nuclear power amid rising energy demand, decarbonization goals, and geopolitical tensions.

stories.title: Cortical Labs unveils data centers powered by human brain cells
stories.description: The Australian biotech startup opened a biological computing facility in Melbourne and is building a second in Singapore with partner DayOne Data Centers.

stories.title: US now weighs SPR oil sales amid Iran war price surge
stories.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the administration is discussing coordinated reserve releases with allies, reversing its stance from days earlier.

stories.title: US rejects Iran's claim that warship sunk by torpedo was unarmed
stories.description: INDOPACOM called Tehran's assertion "false" on Sunday as the two nations spar over the IRIS Dena, sunk March 4 off Sri Lanka, killing at least 84 sailors.

stories.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley leaves GOP, narrowing Johnson's House majority
stories.description: The California congressman becomes the only independent in the House, citing frustration with gerrymandering and hyper-partisanship.

stories.title: Group that beat Trump at Supreme Court sues over new tariffs
stories.description: The Liberty Justice Center filed suit on behalf of two small businesses, calling the president's 10% global tariff under Section 122 unlawful.

stories.title: Pentagon IDs 7th service member killed in Iran war
stories.description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 26, died Sunday from injuries sustained in a March 1 Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Kuwait's emir condemns Iran's 'brutal attack' in first address since strikes began
stories.description: Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah broke his silence as the country buried two more border security officers killed in the conflict.

stories.title: Trump declares SAVE America Act as number one priority
stories.description: The president told House Republicans he won't approve any legislation until his election integrity package passes, threatening the CLARITY Act's timeline.

stories.title: CENTCOM denies Patriot misfire, blames Iranian drone for Bahrain civilian injuries
stories.description: The U.S. called Russian and Iranian media reports that a Patriot missile struck a Bahrain neighborhood a "lie," saying an Iranian drone injured 32 civilians.

stories.title: Explosion damages historic synagogue in Belgian city of Liège
stories.description: Belgian officials condemned the early Monday blast as an antisemitic act targeting the Jewish community. No injuries were reported.

stories.title: Trump says Vance was 'less enthusiastic' about Iran strikes, downplays rift
stories.description: The president publicly acknowledged his vice president's initial reluctance but insisted they agree as the war enters its second week.

stories.title: IAEA chief says Iran's enriched uranium likely survived strikes at Isfahan
stories.description: Grossi told reporters over 200 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium was stored in Isfahan's tunnels before last June's attacks and probably remains there.

stories.title: IRGC offers Hormuz passage to nations that expel US, Israeli envoys
stories.description: Iran ties strait transit rights to a diplomatic ultimatum as France assembles an unprecedented naval force to reopen the waterway.

stories.title: Putin pitches Iran peace plan to Trump, backs new supreme leader
stories.description: On a day of diplomatic maneuvering, the Russian president proposed a swift end to the Iran war while pledging "unwavering support" to Mojtaba Khamenei.

stories.title: RAF Typhoons down two drones over Jordan and Bahrain
stories.description: Defence Secretary Healey told the Commons the UK is now flying defensive sorties over the Gulf as HMS Dragon prepares to deploy.

stories.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
stories.description: A jury found the three wealthy siblings guilty after 11 women testified they were drugged and assaulted over more than a decade.

stories.title: Trump says Iran was building granite-fortified nuclear site
stories.description: The president claimed Iran planned to attack the U.S. "within a week," contradicting what intelligence officials privately told Congress.

stories.title: Iran says it does not want to harm 'ordinary Americans'
stories.description: As the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week, Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran's fight is with "Israel and its dupes in Washington," not ordinary Americans.

stories.title: Sen. Booker proposes making first $75,000 tax-free
stories.description: The Keep Your Pay Act would more than double the standard deduction and expand child and earned income tax credits for working families.

stories.title: Judge strikes down Trump's three-person NJ prosecutor setup
stories.description: A federal judge ruled the triumvirate running the New Jersey U.S. Attorney's Office was unconstitutionally appointed, threatening case dismissals.

stories.title: UAE calls for de-escalation after 1,400 Iranian attacks
stories.description: The Gulf state's UN envoy said four civilians were killed and 114 injured since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on Feb. 28.

stories.title: US intercepts Iran signal that may activate sleeper cells abroad
stories.description: Encrypted transmission detected after Khamenei's death could be an "operational trigger" for covert operatives, a federal alert warns.

stories.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran and Ukraine in phone call
stories.description: The Kremlin said the Monday call, initiated by Trump, covered the Iran conflict and trilateral Ukraine settlement talks.

stories.title: Von der Leyen says 'no tears' for Iran regime, drawing EU diplomat backlash
stories.description: The European Commission president declined to condemn US-Israeli strikes on Iran and faced accusations of overstepping her foreign policy authority.

stories.title: Live Nation settles DOJ antitrust case, keeps Ticketmaster
stories.description: The deal requires amphitheater divestitures, caps on service fees and shorter exclusivity contracts but avoids a forced breakup of the ticketing giant.

stories.title: Trump doesn't rule out seizing Iranian oil as prices spike
stories.description: The president pointed to Venezuela as a precedent, saying "people have talked about it" but that it's "too soon" to discuss.

stories.title: Iran war splits BRICS as India blocks joint response
stories.description: More than a week into the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, the 11-nation bloc has failed to issue a collective statement on a conflict engulfing one of its own members.

stories.title: Musk pushes vision of robot-powered 'sustainable abundance'
stories.description: The Tesla CEO says AI and humanoid robots will make work optional, but economists warn the timeline and wealth distribution remain major obstacles.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war is 'very complete,' claims military destroyed
stories.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader the same day Trump claimed Tehran has lost its navy, air force, and communications.

stories.title: South Korea, Taiwan cap fuel prices as oil tops $100
stories.description: The emergency measures, first in nearly 30 years for South Korea, aim to shield consumers as the Middle East conflict drives crude prices sharply higher.

stories.title: Kansas City airport evacuated after bomb threat
stories.description: About 2,000 travelers were moved onto the tarmac as the FBI and airport police swept the terminal and flights were halted nationwide.

stories.title: Trump sons back drone firm Powerus in bid for Pentagon contracts
stories.description: Powerus plans to go public via a reverse merger and pursue the Pentagon's $1.1 billion Drone Dominance program, the WSJ reported.

stories.title: Israel says it killed Iran's new military bureau chief in Tehran strike
stories.description: Baba'iyan was appointed days earlier after his predecessor was killed in the opening strike of Operation Roaring Lion.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia reports first deaths as projectile hits Al-Kharj
stories.description: An Indian and a Bangladeshi national were killed and 12 others injured when a military projectile struck a residential compound on Sunday.

stories.title: Trump rebuffs UK carrier readiness, says US 'already won' Iran war
stories.description: The president mocked Starmer's move to ready HMS Prince of Wales, warning on Truth Social that the U.S. "will remember" the UK's delayed support.

stories.title: UAE denies Israeli reports it struck Iranian desalination plant
stories.description: Emirati officials called the claims "fake news," saying the UAE has the courage to announce its own military actions.

stories.title: Israeli settlers murder three Palestinians in West Bank village raid
stories.description: Six Palestinians have been killed by settlers in the past week as violence escalates across the occupied West Bank during Ramadan.

stories.title: Trump says ending Iran war will be 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu
stories.description: As fighting enters its tenth day, Iran named the slain supreme leader's son as his successor, drawing a warning from Trump.

stories.title: Renewed MH370 search yields no findings 12 years after plane vanished
stories.description: Malaysia's AAIB confirmed the 28-day deep-sea search by Ocean Infinity covered over 7,500 sq km of seabed but found no trace of the aircraft.

stories.title: Iranian drone damages Bahrain desalination plant as war enters ninth day
stories.description: Bahrain's interior ministry says the strike marks the first time a Gulf state has reported Iranian damage to critical water infrastructure in the conflict.

stories.title: Health experts urge caution as daylight saving time returns Sunday
stories.description: Research links the spring time change to higher rates of heart attacks, strokes, fatal car crashes, and workplace injuries in the days that follow.

stories.title: Judge voids Kari Lake's Voice of America cuts, rules her tenure unlawful
stories.description: A federal judge declared all actions Lake took as acting USAGM CEO "null and void," finding her appointment violated the Federal Vacancies Reform Act.

stories.title: US Embassy in Baghdad attacked as Iran war enters second week
stories.description: Katyusha rockets struck the Green Zone in the first embassy attack since the conflict began, while the UAE intercepted another massive wave of missiles and drones.

stories.title: Ukraine says it halted Russia's advance toward Zaporizhzhia in three-month operation
stories.description: HUR claims its special forces killed or wounded over 300 Russian soldiers and captured 39 more while stabilizing defensive lines in the south.

stories.title: Trump formally recognizes Venezuela's Rodriguez government
stories.description: The announcement came at the Shield of the Americas Summit in Florida, alongside a new deal for Venezuelan gold and mineral exports to the U.S.

stories.title: Guinea dissolves 40 political parties, including top opposition groups
stories.description: The late-night decree strips parties of legal status and seizes their assets less than two months after former junta leader Doumbouya's inauguration.

stories.title: US deploys third aircraft carrier USS George HW Bush to Middle East
stories.description: The deployment would create the largest American naval aviation presence in the Middle East since the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

stories.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to flag humanities grants for cancellation, court filings reveal
stories.description: Discovery documents show the efficiency office fed grant descriptions into the AI chatbot to label projects as "DEI," bypassing NEH staff.

stories.title: Protests erupt across Havana after days without power
stories.description: Cubans banged pots and shouted slogans in darkened streets Friday night after enduring up to 80 hours without electricity amid a deepening energy crisis.

stories.title: Balen Shah's party heads for landslide in Nepal's post-protest election
stories.description: The rapper-turned-politician defeated ex-PM KP Sharma Oli and his RSP leads in over 100 constituencies, shattering traditional party dominance.

stories.title: Trump doesn't rule out ground troops in Iran
stories.description: Speaking aboard Air Force One after honoring six fallen soldiers at Dover, the president said deploying troops to secure enriched uranium "is something we could consider later."

stories.title: US clears $152M emergency arms sale to Israel, bypassing Congress
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio invoked emergency powers to expedite 12,000 bomb bodies to Israel as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran intensifies.

stories.title: US suspects Chinese hackers breached FBI wiretap network
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that hackers linked to China's government infiltrated an FBI system holding domestic surveillance data.

stories.title: US military carries out lethal strikes in Ecuador
stories.description: The Pentagon confirmed Friday's operations targeted a narco-terrorist supply complex, escalating from an advisory role that began days earlier.

stories.title: Chinese study on US missile defense gaps validated by Iran war, analysts say
stories.description: Iran's hypersonic missiles have repeatedly penetrated THAAD and other advanced US defense systems since the conflict began Feb. 28.

stories.title: Cuba shuts Quito embassy after Ecuador expels diplomats
stories.description: The closure came after a 48-hour ultimatum expired, marking one of Latin America's sharpest diplomatic ruptures in years.

stories.title: Iran fires new missile waves at Tel Aviv
stories.description: The IRGC launched its 21st and 22nd waves of strikes Friday, deploying Khorramshahr-4, Kheibar, and Fattah missiles at central Israel.

stories.title: Trump honors six US troops killed in Kuwait at Dover ceremony
stories.description: The ceremony marked the first dignified transfer since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran on Feb. 28, confronting Trump with costs of a war he initiated.

stories.title: Iran warns Europe of retaliation if nations join US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Takht-Ravanchi issued the threat as France, Britain, and Italy accelerate military deployments across the Mediterranean and Gulf.

stories.title: UN chief warns Iran conflict could spiral out of control
stories.description: Guterres condemned "unlawful attacks" across the Middle East and called for immediate diplomatic negotiations as the war enters its second week.

stories.title: Pentagon sends Ukraine-tested Merops anti-drone system to Middle East
stories.description: Officials admit U.S. forces lack adequate defenses against Iranian drone waves targeting personnel in the Gulf region.

stories.title: Pakistani man convicted in Iran-backed plot to kill Trump, US politicians
stories.description: A federal jury in Brooklyn found Asif Merchant guilty of conspiring to assassinate U.S. politicians in a scheme prosecutors tied to Iran's IRGC.

stories.title: Bessent says US may ease more Russia oil sanctions
stories.description: The Treasury secretary's comments came a day after Washington granted India a 30-day waiver to buy sanctioned Russian crude stranded at sea.

stories.title: Judge skeptical of Pentagon rules barring unapproved reporting by journalists
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sharply questioned the government's defense of press restrictions, telling the courtroom "asking a question is not criminal."

stories.title: Trump says defense firms agreed to quadruple advanced weapons production
stories.description: The announcement followed a White House meeting with six major contractors as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week.

stories.title: US, Ukraine discuss free-trade pact and recovery fund, minister says
stories.description: Ukraine's economy minister said negotiators are working on a trade agreement and sovereign fund as part of a broader postwar prosperity package.

stories.title: Newly revealed DEA probe linked Epstein to ketamine trade
stories.description: A newly detailed investigation called "Operation Chain Reaction" tracked roughly $50 million in suspicious wire transfers tied to drug trafficking and money laundering.

stories.title: Israel bombing western Iran to back Kurdish offensive
stories.description: Sources say thousands of Kurdish fighters are massing on the Iraqi border, preparing to seize Iranian towns within a week.

stories.title: Lewandowski leaves DHS after Noem's firing as secretary
stories.description: Trump adviser Corey Lewandowski wielded outsized power as Noem's unofficial chief of staff despite holding unpaid, part-time status.

stories.title: US push for Venezuela mining access threatens environmental crisis
stories.description: The Interior secretary met with Venezuela's acting president and said the government offered security guarantees for companies entering mineral-rich regions.

stories.title: US retail sales fell in January, extending trend
stories.description: Consumer spending dropped broadly, with department stores, personal care outlets, and gas stations leading the declines.

stories.title: UK arrests four men suspected of spying on Jewish sites for Iran
stories.description: Counter-terrorism police detained one Iranian national and three dual British-Iranian citizens in a pre-dawn operation across London.

stories.title: Iceland sets Aug. 29 referendum on resuming EU talks
stories.description: The government unanimously agreed to fast-track the vote, originally pledged for 2027, amid security concerns tied to Trump's Greenland threats.

stories.title: US economy sheds 92,000 jobs in February, defying forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% as employers cut payrolls instead of adding the 59,000 jobs economists had expected.

stories.title: Iran reports 1,332 civilian deaths in UN letter on sixth day of war
stories.description: Tehran's UN envoy urged the Security Council to take "urgent, collective measures" to halt US-Israeli strikes on Iranian territory.

stories.title: Axel Springer agrees to buy UK's Telegraph for £575M
stories.description: The German media giant's deal upends the Daily Mail owner's planned acquisition and places the 168-year-old conservative paper under foreign ownership.

stories.title: Jimmy Lai won't appeal 20-year Hong Kong sentence
stories.description: The 78-year-old Apple Daily founder's legal team confirmed the decision without explanation, closing one of Hong Kong's most prominent national security cases.

stories.title: Texas Rep. Tony Gonzales ends reelection bid amid ethics probe
stories.description: The Texas Republican withdrew hours after House Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP leadership publicly called on him to end his campaign.

stories.title: SoftBank seeks record $40B loan to fund OpenAI bet
stories.description: The Japanese conglomerate is in talks with JPMorgan and three other banks for a 12-month bridge loan, according to Bloomberg.

stories.title: DOJ releases withheld Epstein files tied to Trump allegations
stories.description: The department said three FBI interview memos were "incorrectly coded as duplicative" and left out of the public archive.

stories.title: US reports 90 percent drop in Iranian ballistic missile attacks
stories.description: CENTCOM's commander said B-2 bombers struck nearly 200 targets deep inside Iran in the past 72 hours as offensive operations shift to dismantling Tehran's production capacity.

stories.title: UAE weighs freezing billions in Iranian assets
stories.description: The move would target Tehran's financial networks in Dubai as Iranian missile and drone strikes on the Gulf state enter a second week.

stories.title: Noem bypassed bidding on $100M ICE contracts, NBC reports
stories.description: The outgoing DHS secretary handpicked the same politically connected firms already under scrutiny for a separate $220 million ad campaign.

stories.title: Iraq's first lady tells US to 'leave the Kurds alone'
stories.description: Shanaz Ibrahim Ahmed warned against enlisting Kurdish fighters as proxies in the war against Iran, saying "we are not guns for hire."

top.title: Synopsys debuts AI chip design tools at Converge 2026
top.description: The launches mark the first major product deliveries since Synopsys completed its $35 billion acquisition of Ansys last year.

top.title: Oil prices climb again as nations plan historic reserve release
top.description: Crude rose more than 3% Wednesday despite governments signaling emergency stockpile releases amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

top.title: U.S. military probe finds America at fault in Iran school strike that killed 175
top.description: Outdated targeting data led to the Tomahawk missile hitting a girls' elementary school on the war's first day, according to preliminary findings.

top.title: Norway arrests three brothers over U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo
top.description: The suspects, all Norwegian citizens of Iraqi background in their twenties, face terror bombing charges under the penal code.

top.title: Kilauea's 1,000-foot lava fountains force closures on Big Island
top.description: Episode 43 of the ongoing eruption showered communities with volcanic debris, closing Highway 11 and parts of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park.

top.title: Trump says 'practically nothing left' to target in Iran
top.description: Israel's defense minister vowed to fight on "without any time limit," while Iran warned of a prolonged war of attrition.

top.title: IRGC claims strikes on ships in Strait of Hormuz, demands transit approval
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it targeted vessels that ignored warnings, as three cargo ships were hit by projectiles on Wednesday.

top.title: IEA confirms largest-ever oil reserve release at 400M barrels
top.description: The unprecedented action aims to contain surging energy prices as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran has effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Anthropic launches policy institute amid Pentagon legal battle
top.description: Co-founder Jack Clark will lead the Anthropic Institute, a new research body studying AI's impact on jobs, law, and the economy.

top.title: Trump to visit Ohio, Kentucky to sell economic message and campaign against Massie
top.description: The president's two-stop swing marks the first time this cycle he has personally campaigned to oust a sitting Republican member of Congress.

top.title: Iran's president's son says new leader is 'safe and sound' amid injury reports
top.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since being named supreme leader on March 9, fueling speculation about wounds from U.S.-Israeli strikes.

top.title: Von der Leyen calls return to Russian energy 'a strategic blunder' amid Iran war
top.description: The European Commission president told the European Parliament that 10 days of conflict have already cost EU taxpayers an extra €3 billion in fossil fuel imports.

top.title: US gas prices top $3.50 as Iran war disrupts Strait of Hormuz oil flow
top.description: The national average hit $3.54 per gallon as of March 10, up more than 60 cents in a month, with California topping $5.29.

top.title: UN Security Council to vote on resolution demanding Iran halt Gulf attacks
top.description: The Gulf-backed measure, with over 90 co-sponsors, condemns Iran's strikes on neighbors and its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: New AI tools compress months of drug and protein research into days
top.description: From drug synthesis to protein mapping, multiple studies published in recent weeks show machine learning dramatically cutting lab time and costs.

top.title: Trial opens for 4 men charged in Haiti president's assassination
top.description: Prosecutors told the jury that greed and a thirst for power drove the South Florida-based conspiracy to kill Jovenel Moïse in 2021.

top.title: AG Bondi relocates to military base amid cartel, Epstein threats
top.description: The attorney general quietly left her D.C. apartment for a nearby military installation, joining a growing list of Trump officials seeking base housing.

top.title: Experts accuse Bukele's government of crimes against humanity
top.description: International experts filed a complaint with the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleging systematic torture, killings, and disappearances under El Salvador's four-year emergency rule.

top.title: Fuller and Harris head to Georgia runoff for Greene seat
top.description: Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller will face off April 7 for the deep-red 14th District seat.

top.title: Pentagon orders removal of Anthropic AI from military systems
top.description: An internal memo directs commanders to strip Claude from nuclear, missile defense, and cyber warfare systems as Anthropic sues the Trump administration.

top.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Haifa refinery in second attack in three days
top.description: The Tuesday strikes targeted oil, gas, and fuel storage facilities at Israel's largest refinery, deepening a tit-for-tat energy infrastructure war.

top.title: Hyde-Smith wins Mississippi GOP Senate primary
top.description: The Trump-endorsed incumbent defeated challenger Sarah Adlakha with roughly 83% of the vote, advancing to the November general election.

top.title: Johnson opposes nation-building in Iran, breaks with Trump
top.description: The House speaker said rebuilding Iran is "not our responsibility," a day after the president called the war "the beginning of building a new country."

top.title: Sen. Blumenthal warns US is on path to ground troops in Iran
top.description: After a classified briefing, the senator said Russia appears to be "actively and intensively" aiding Iran as the Pentagon reported 140 U.S. troops wounded.

top.title: Israel says it's not seeking 'endless war' as strikes on Tehran intensify
top.description: Foreign Minister Sa'ar pledged to coordinate with the U.S. on ending the conflict but offered no timeline on day 11 of fighting.

top.title: IRGC says Iran will decide when war ends, threatens Gulf oil blockade
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards dismissed Trump's claims the conflict is nearly over, warning they would halt all regional oil exports.

top.title: Sen. Kennedy apologizes for US strike on Iranian girls' school that killed 168
top.description: The Louisiana Republican broke with Trump, calling the Feb. 28 bombing of the Minab school a "mistake" and expressing sorrow for the deaths.

top.title: Merz calls Israel's West Bank settlement push a 'big mistake'
top.description: Germany's foreign minister made a surprise visit to Jerusalem to deliver the message directly, the first by a European diplomat since the Iran war began.

top.title: US Navy refuses escort requests through Hormuz
top.description: The Navy has told shipping companies daily that attacks risks are too high for escorts, even as Trump promises tanker protection.

top.title: Hegseth warns Russia against sharing intel with Iran
top.description: Iran's foreign minister confirmed Moscow is "helping us in many different directions" as the U.S. accuses Russia of aiding Tehran with military intelligence.

top.title: Tankers begin running Strait of Hormuz blockade as US backs $20B reinsurance plan
top.description: A handful of oil tankers have broken through the effective Hormuz blockade, but daily transits remain near zero as Iran warns vessels to stay away.

top.title: White House urges GOP to drop 'mass deportation' talk
top.description: Deputy Chief of Staff James Blair told House Republicans to shift messaging toward removing violent criminals as polls show eroding support.

top.title: Senate confirms Joshua Rudd to lead NSA and Cyber Command
top.description: The 71-29 vote ends a nearly year-long leadership vacancy atop the nation's top signals intelligence agency and military cyber operations arm.

top.title: Pentagon warns of 'most intense' day of strikes on Iran
top.description: On day 11 of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, Defense Secretary Hegseth said Tuesday would see the most fighters, bombers, and strikes yet deployed.

top.title: Trump announces first new US oil refinery in 50 years
top.description: India's Reliance Industries is backing the Brownsville, Texas, facility, which will process domestic shale oil into gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel.

top.title: Musk says X Money will enter early public access in April
top.description: The digital payment system, built into the X platform, offers P2P transfers, a Visa debit card, and a 6% yield on deposits.

top.title: Up to 150 US troops wounded in Iran war, Pentagon says
top.description: The figure, disclosed after a Reuters report, far exceeds the eight serious injuries the Pentagon had previously acknowledged publicly.

top.title: Witkoff likely to visit Israel next week to coordinate Iran war plans
top.description: Trump's special envoy plans to visit Israel next week to coordinate the ongoing military campaign as war with Iran enters its second week.

top.title: Ukraine says leaked Russian documents show 1.3M war losses
top.description: Classified reports obtained by Ukrainian intelligence put Russia's own count of killed and seriously wounded at 1.315 million, Zelenskyy said.

top.title: Iran war fuels global debate over shift to renewables
top.description: EU ministers began emergency energy talks as oil price swings and disrupted shipping routes revive calls for clean energy as a security imperative.

top.title: NRC proposes cutting nuclear safety inspections by 38% under Trump orders
top.description: Emergency preparedness inspections face the steepest cuts at 56%, with security and radiation protection hours also sharply reduced.

top.title: Shots fired at US Consulate in Toronto as attacks on American posts spread
top.description: Police found roughly 10 shell casings and a damaged door but no injuries after a predawn shooting at the consulate on University Avenue.

top.title: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square files for $5B dual IPO on NYSE
top.description: The combined offering would list both a new closed-end fund and Ackman's management company, with shares priced at $50 apiece.

top.title: Trump advisers privately push for Iran exit strategy amid surging gas prices
top.description: National gas prices have jumped more than 60 cents in a month as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupts oil flows, raising GOP midterm fears.

top.title: Netanyahu vows to keep striking Iran, says campaign is 'breaking their bones'
top.description: Israel has tripled its strike tempo since the U.S.-Israeli offensive began Feb. 28, while Iran vows to fight on and rejects any ceasefire.

top.title: Lawmakers in 11 states propose temporary bans on data centers
top.description: Bipartisan legislation targets new data center construction over concerns about soaring electricity costs and water use driven by the AI boom.

top.title: Apple now assembles 25% of iPhones in India
top.description: India-made iPhones hit 55 million units in 2025, a 53% jump, as Apple accelerates its manufacturing shift away from China.

top.title: ALMA detects high methanol levels in interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
top.description: The third known interstellar object to pass through our solar system has a chemical makeup unlike nearly any comet born here, researchers say.

top.title: USDA monitors fertilizer market for price gouging amid Iran war supply crisis
top.description: The department is watching for unfair markups as the Strait of Hormuz closure chokes off shipments weeks before spring planting.

top.title: Iran's FM rules out new talks with US, citing attacks during negotiations
top.description: Araghchi told PBS News that diplomacy with Washington is off the table after the US launched strikes days after both sides reported progress in nuclear talks.

top.title: Macron, IAEA chief open nuclear energy summit in Paris
top.description: World leaders gathered to discuss expanding civilian nuclear power amid rising energy demand, decarbonization goals, and geopolitical tensions.

top.title: Cortical Labs unveils data centers powered by human brain cells
top.description: The Australian biotech startup opened a biological computing facility in Melbourne and is building a second in Singapore with partner DayOne Data Centers.

top.title: US now weighs SPR oil sales amid Iran war price surge
top.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the administration is discussing coordinated reserve releases with allies, reversing its stance from days earlier.

top.title: US rejects Iran's claim that warship sunk by torpedo was unarmed
top.description: INDOPACOM called Tehran's assertion "false" on Sunday as the two nations spar over the IRIS Dena, sunk March 4 off Sri Lanka, killing at least 84 sailors.

top.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley leaves GOP, narrowing Johnson's House majority
top.description: The California congressman becomes the only independent in the House, citing frustration with gerrymandering and hyper-partisanship.

top.title: Group that beat Trump at Supreme Court sues over new tariffs
top.description: The Liberty Justice Center filed suit on behalf of two small businesses, calling the president's 10% global tariff under Section 122 unlawful.

top.title: Pentagon IDs 7th service member killed in Iran war
top.description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 26, died Sunday from injuries sustained in a March 1 Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia.

top.title: Kuwait's emir condemns Iran's 'brutal attack' in first address since strikes began
top.description: Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah broke his silence as the country buried two more border security officers killed in the conflict.

top.title: Trump declares SAVE America Act as number one priority
top.description: The president told House Republicans he won't approve any legislation until his election integrity package passes, threatening the CLARITY Act's timeline.

top.title: CENTCOM denies Patriot misfire, blames Iranian drone for Bahrain civilian injuries
top.description: The U.S. called Russian and Iranian media reports that a Patriot missile struck a Bahrain neighborhood a "lie," saying an Iranian drone injured 32 civilians.

top.title: Explosion damages historic synagogue in Belgian city of Liège
top.description: Belgian officials condemned the early Monday blast as an antisemitic act targeting the Jewish community. No injuries were reported.

top.title: Trump says Vance was 'less enthusiastic' about Iran strikes, downplays rift
top.description: The president publicly acknowledged his vice president's initial reluctance but insisted they agree as the war enters its second week.

top.title: IAEA chief says Iran's enriched uranium likely survived strikes at Isfahan
top.description: Grossi told reporters over 200 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium was stored in Isfahan's tunnels before last June's attacks and probably remains there.

top.title: IRGC offers Hormuz passage to nations that expel US, Israeli envoys
top.description: Iran ties strait transit rights to a diplomatic ultimatum as France assembles an unprecedented naval force to reopen the waterway.

top.title: Putin pitches Iran peace plan to Trump, backs new supreme leader
top.description: On a day of diplomatic maneuvering, the Russian president proposed a swift end to the Iran war while pledging "unwavering support" to Mojtaba Khamenei.

top.title: RAF Typhoons down two drones over Jordan and Bahrain
top.description: Defence Secretary Healey told the Commons the UK is now flying defensive sorties over the Gulf as HMS Dragon prepares to deploy.

top.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
top.description: A jury found the three wealthy siblings guilty after 11 women testified they were drugged and assaulted over more than a decade.

top.title: Trump says Iran was building granite-fortified nuclear site
top.description: The president claimed Iran planned to attack the U.S. "within a week," contradicting what intelligence officials privately told Congress.

top.title: Iran says it does not want to harm 'ordinary Americans'
top.description: As the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week, Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran's fight is with "Israel and its dupes in Washington," not ordinary Americans.

top.title: Sen. Booker proposes making first $75,000 tax-free
top.description: The Keep Your Pay Act would more than double the standard deduction and expand child and earned income tax credits for working families.

top.title: Judge strikes down Trump's three-person NJ prosecutor setup
top.description: A federal judge ruled the triumvirate running the New Jersey U.S. Attorney's Office was unconstitutionally appointed, threatening case dismissals.

top.title: UAE calls for de-escalation after 1,400 Iranian attacks
top.description: The Gulf state's UN envoy said four civilians were killed and 114 injured since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on Feb. 28.

top.title: US intercepts Iran signal that may activate sleeper cells abroad
top.description: Encrypted transmission detected after Khamenei's death could be an "operational trigger" for covert operatives, a federal alert warns.

top.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran and Ukraine in phone call
top.description: The Kremlin said the Monday call, initiated by Trump, covered the Iran conflict and trilateral Ukraine settlement talks.

top.title: Von der Leyen says 'no tears' for Iran regime, drawing EU diplomat backlash
top.description: The European Commission president declined to condemn US-Israeli strikes on Iran and faced accusations of overstepping her foreign policy authority.

top.title: Live Nation settles DOJ antitrust case, keeps Ticketmaster
top.description: The deal requires amphitheater divestitures, caps on service fees and shorter exclusivity contracts but avoids a forced breakup of the ticketing giant.

top.title: Trump doesn't rule out seizing Iranian oil as prices spike
top.description: The president pointed to Venezuela as a precedent, saying "people have talked about it" but that it's "too soon" to discuss.

top.title: Iran war splits BRICS as India blocks joint response
top.description: More than a week into the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, the 11-nation bloc has failed to issue a collective statement on a conflict engulfing one of its own members.

top.title: Musk pushes vision of robot-powered 'sustainable abundance'
top.description: The Tesla CEO says AI and humanoid robots will make work optional, but economists warn the timeline and wealth distribution remain major obstacles.

top.title: Trump says Iran war is 'very complete,' claims military destroyed
top.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader the same day Trump claimed Tehran has lost its navy, air force, and communications.

top.title: South Korea, Taiwan cap fuel prices as oil tops $100
top.description: The emergency measures, first in nearly 30 years for South Korea, aim to shield consumers as the Middle East conflict drives crude prices sharply higher.

top.title: Kansas City airport evacuated after bomb threat
top.description: About 2,000 travelers were moved onto the tarmac as the FBI and airport police swept the terminal and flights were halted nationwide.

top.title: Trump sons back drone firm Powerus in bid for Pentagon contracts
top.description: Powerus plans to go public via a reverse merger and pursue the Pentagon's $1.1 billion Drone Dominance program, the WSJ reported.

top.title: Israel says it killed Iran's new military bureau chief in Tehran strike
top.description: Baba'iyan was appointed days earlier after his predecessor was killed in the opening strike of Operation Roaring Lion.

top.title: Saudi Arabia reports first deaths as projectile hits Al-Kharj
top.description: An Indian and a Bangladeshi national were killed and 12 others injured when a military projectile struck a residential compound on Sunday.

top.title: Trump rebuffs UK carrier readiness, says US 'already won' Iran war
top.description: The president mocked Starmer's move to ready HMS Prince of Wales, warning on Truth Social that the U.S. "will remember" the UK's delayed support.

top.title: UAE denies Israeli reports it struck Iranian desalination plant
top.description: Emirati officials called the claims "fake news," saying the UAE has the courage to announce its own military actions.

top.title: Israeli settlers murder three Palestinians in West Bank village raid
top.description: Six Palestinians have been killed by settlers in the past week as violence escalates across the occupied West Bank during Ramadan.

top.title: Trump says ending Iran war will be 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu
top.description: As fighting enters its tenth day, Iran named the slain supreme leader's son as his successor, drawing a warning from Trump.

top.title: Renewed MH370 search yields no findings 12 years after plane vanished
top.description: Malaysia's AAIB confirmed the 28-day deep-sea search by Ocean Infinity covered over 7,500 sq km of seabed but found no trace of the aircraft.

top.title: Iranian drone damages Bahrain desalination plant as war enters ninth day
top.description: Bahrain's interior ministry says the strike marks the first time a Gulf state has reported Iranian damage to critical water infrastructure in the conflict.

top.title: Health experts urge caution as daylight saving time returns Sunday
top.description: Research links the spring time change to higher rates of heart attacks, strokes, fatal car crashes, and workplace injuries in the days that follow.

top.title: Judge voids Kari Lake's Voice of America cuts, rules her tenure unlawful
top.description: A federal judge declared all actions Lake took as acting USAGM CEO "null and void," finding her appointment violated the Federal Vacancies Reform Act.

top.title: US Embassy in Baghdad attacked as Iran war enters second week
top.description: Katyusha rockets struck the Green Zone in the first embassy attack since the conflict began, while the UAE intercepted another massive wave of missiles and drones.

top.title: Ukraine says it halted Russia's advance toward Zaporizhzhia in three-month operation
top.description: HUR claims its special forces killed or wounded over 300 Russian soldiers and captured 39 more while stabilizing defensive lines in the south.

top.title: Trump formally recognizes Venezuela's Rodriguez government
top.description: The announcement came at the Shield of the Americas Summit in Florida, alongside a new deal for Venezuelan gold and mineral exports to the U.S.

top.title: Guinea dissolves 40 political parties, including top opposition groups
top.description: The late-night decree strips parties of legal status and seizes their assets less than two months after former junta leader Doumbouya's inauguration.

top.title: US deploys third aircraft carrier USS George HW Bush to Middle East
top.description: The deployment would create the largest American naval aviation presence in the Middle East since the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

top.title: DOGE used ChatGPT to flag humanities grants for cancellation, court filings reveal
top.description: Discovery documents show the efficiency office fed grant descriptions into the AI chatbot to label projects as "DEI," bypassing NEH staff.

top.title: Protests erupt across Havana after days without power
top.description: Cubans banged pots and shouted slogans in darkened streets Friday night after enduring up to 80 hours without electricity amid a deepening energy crisis.

top.title: Balen Shah's party heads for landslide in Nepal's post-protest election
top.description: The rapper-turned-politician defeated ex-PM KP Sharma Oli and his RSP leads in over 100 constituencies, shattering traditional party dominance.

top.title: Trump doesn't rule out ground troops in Iran
top.description: Speaking aboard Air Force One after honoring six fallen soldiers at Dover, the president said deploying troops to secure enriched uranium "is something we could consider later."

top.title: US clears $152M emergency arms sale to Israel, bypassing Congress
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio invoked emergency powers to expedite 12,000 bomb bodies to Israel as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran intensifies.

top.title: US suspects Chinese hackers breached FBI wiretap network
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that hackers linked to China's government infiltrated an FBI system holding domestic surveillance data.

top.title: US military carries out lethal strikes in Ecuador
top.description: The Pentagon confirmed Friday's operations targeted a narco-terrorist supply complex, escalating from an advisory role that began days earlier.

top.title: Chinese study on US missile defense gaps validated by Iran war, analysts say
top.description: Iran's hypersonic missiles have repeatedly penetrated THAAD and other advanced US defense systems since the conflict began Feb. 28.

top.title: Cuba shuts Quito embassy after Ecuador expels diplomats
top.description: The closure came after a 48-hour ultimatum expired, marking one of Latin America's sharpest diplomatic ruptures in years.

top.title: Iran fires new missile waves at Tel Aviv
top.description: The IRGC launched its 21st and 22nd waves of strikes Friday, deploying Khorramshahr-4, Kheibar, and Fattah missiles at central Israel.

top.title: Trump honors six US troops killed in Kuwait at Dover ceremony
top.description: The ceremony marked the first dignified transfer since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran on Feb. 28, confronting Trump with costs of a war he initiated.

top.title: Iran warns Europe of retaliation if nations join US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Takht-Ravanchi issued the threat as France, Britain, and Italy accelerate military deployments across the Mediterranean and Gulf.

top.title: UN chief warns Iran conflict could spiral out of control
top.description: Guterres condemned "unlawful attacks" across the Middle East and called for immediate diplomatic negotiations as the war enters its second week.

top.title: Pentagon sends Ukraine-tested Merops anti-drone system to Middle East
top.description: Officials admit U.S. forces lack adequate defenses against Iranian drone waves targeting personnel in the Gulf region.

top.title: Pakistani man convicted in Iran-backed plot to kill Trump, US politicians
top.description: A federal jury in Brooklyn found Asif Merchant guilty of conspiring to assassinate U.S. politicians in a scheme prosecutors tied to Iran's IRGC.

top.title: Bessent says US may ease more Russia oil sanctions
top.description: The Treasury secretary's comments came a day after Washington granted India a 30-day waiver to buy sanctioned Russian crude stranded at sea.

top.title: Judge skeptical of Pentagon rules barring unapproved reporting by journalists
top.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sharply questioned the government's defense of press restrictions, telling the courtroom "asking a question is not criminal."

top.title: Trump says defense firms agreed to quadruple advanced weapons production
top.description: The announcement followed a White House meeting with six major contractors as the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week.

top.title: US, Ukraine discuss free-trade pact and recovery fund, minister says
top.description: Ukraine's economy minister said negotiators are working on a trade agreement and sovereign fund as part of a broader postwar prosperity package.

top.title: Newly revealed DEA probe linked Epstein to ketamine trade
top.description: A newly detailed investigation called "Operation Chain Reaction" tracked roughly $50 million in suspicious wire transfers tied to drug trafficking and money laundering.

top.title: Israel bombing western Iran to back Kurdish offensive
top.description: Sources say thousands of Kurdish fighters are massing on the Iraqi border, preparing to seize Iranian towns within a week.

top.title: Lewandowski leaves DHS after Noem's firing as secretary
top.description: Trump adviser Corey Lewandowski wielded outsized power as Noem's unofficial chief of staff despite holding unpaid, part-time status.

top.title: US push for Venezuela mining access threatens environmental crisis
top.description: The Interior secretary met with Venezuela's acting president and said the government offered security guarantees for companies entering mineral-rich regions.

top.title: US retail sales fell in January, extending trend
top.description: Consumer spending dropped broadly, with department stores, personal care outlets, and gas stations leading the declines.

top.title: UK arrests four men suspected of spying on Jewish sites for Iran
top.description: Counter-terrorism police detained one Iranian national and three dual British-Iranian citizens in a pre-dawn operation across London.

top.title: Iceland sets Aug. 29 referendum on resuming EU talks
top.description: The government unanimously agreed to fast-track the vote, originally pledged for 2027, amid security concerns tied to Trump's Greenland threats.

top.title: US economy sheds 92,000 jobs in February, defying forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% as employers cut payrolls instead of adding the 59,000 jobs economists had expected.

top.title: Iran reports 1,332 civilian deaths in UN letter on sixth day of war
top.description: Tehran's UN envoy urged the Security Council to take "urgent, collective measures" to halt US-Israeli strikes on Iranian territory.

top.title: Axel Springer agrees to buy UK's Telegraph for £575M
top.description: The German media giant's deal upends the Daily Mail owner's planned acquisition and places the 168-year-old conservative paper under foreign ownership.

top.title: Jimmy Lai won't appeal 20-year Hong Kong sentence
top.description: The 78-year-old Apple Daily founder's legal team confirmed the decision without explanation, closing one of Hong Kong's most prominent national security cases.

top.title: Texas Rep. Tony Gonzales ends reelection bid amid ethics probe
top.description: The Texas Republican withdrew hours after House Speaker Mike Johnson and GOP leadership publicly called on him to end his campaign.

top.title: SoftBank seeks record $40B loan to fund OpenAI bet
top.description: The Japanese conglomerate is in talks with JPMorgan and three other banks for a 12-month bridge loan, according to Bloomberg.

top.title: DOJ releases withheld Epstein files tied to Trump allegations
top.description: The department said three FBI interview memos were "incorrectly coded as duplicative" and left out of the public archive.

top.title: US reports 90 percent drop in Iranian ballistic missile attacks
top.description: CENTCOM's commander said B-2 bombers struck nearly 200 targets deep inside Iran in the past 72 hours as offensive operations shift to dismantling Tehran's production capacity.

top.title: UAE weighs freezing billions in Iranian assets
top.description: The move would target Tehran's financial networks in Dubai as Iranian missile and drone strikes on the Gulf state enter a second week.

top.title: Noem bypassed bidding on $100M ICE contracts, NBC reports
top.description: The outgoing DHS secretary handpicked the same politically connected firms already under scrutiny for a separate $220 million ad campaign.

top.title: Iraq's first lady tells US to 'leave the Kurds alone'
top.description: Shanaz Ibrahim Ahmed warned against enlisting Kurdish fighters as proxies in the war against Iran, saying "we are not guns for hire."
